things.name: Stirling Castle
things.description: One of Scotland's grandest castles, former seat of the Stuart monarchs, with the spectacular Renaissance Royal Palace and Great Hall

things.name: National Wallace Monument
things.description: Victorian tower on Abbey Craig commemorating William Wallace, hero of the Battle of Stirling Bridge — climb 246 steps for extraordinary views

things.name: Bannockburn Heritage Centre (NTS)
things.description: Immersive visitor centre marking the site of Robert the Bruce's victory over Edward II in 1314 — Scotland's most significant medieval battle

things.name: Old Town Jail
things.description: Victorian prison brought back to life with live actor-guided tours through cells and the execution courtyard

things.name: Church of the Holy Rude
things.description: Medieval church where the infant James VI was crowned and John Knox preached — the only church in Scotland besides Westminster Abbey with a royal coronation

things.name: Stirling Smith Art Gallery & Museum
things.description: Stirling's main museum with extensive collections spanning local history, fine art and the world's oldest curling stone

things.name: Doune Castle
things.description: 14th-century castle made famous as Monty Python's Castle Anthrax and a filming location for Outlander and Game of Thrones

things.name: Cambuskenneth Abbey (ruins)
things.description: Riverside abbey ruins where James III and his queen are buried — an atmospheric walk from Stirling city centre

things.name: The Trossachs & Loch Lomond (Day Trip)
things.description: Gateway to the beautiful Trossachs National Park — explore Loch Katrine, Ben Venue and the Rob Roy heritage country

things.name: Argyll's Lodging
things.description: Scotland's most complete surviving Renaissance town mansion, adjacent to Stirling Castle — now a luxury hostel
